response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, some C/C++, some Java), but the role requires deep JVM expertise, strong Kotlin, and hands-on Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience — none of which appear meaningfully in the resume. Past National Instruments work touched a C IDE but not JVM/Kotlin tooling. The fit for an enterprise JVM IDE role is weak despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: Long track record across complex, large-scale systems and developer tooling
Prior exposure to IDE/compiler-adjacent work at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Strong testing/craftsmanship discipline (TDD, refactoring dojos, fluent-asserts library)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only superficial Java mentions
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework experience listed
Primary recent work is Node/TypeScript and DLang, not JVM tooling or IDE internals

key_factors.factor: Lack of Spring/JVM enterprise framework experience
Some IDE/tooling and language-internals exposure (LabWindows/CVI, DLang tooling)
